target.name: SambaNova Systems
target.founder: Rodrigo Liang, Kunle Olukotun, Christopher Ré

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 11 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
SambaNova Systems is a Series D frontier-chip company valued at $5.0B post-money, having raised $1.1B to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 11 same-sector comparables in Chips & Semiconductors, spanning 3 public companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Tenstorrent ($2.7B), PsiQuantum ($7.0B), and Cerebras ($23.0B).

Two triangulation methods apply to the target. Stage-progression analysis uses the standard Series D step-up multiplier of 1.5x against the $5.0B last post-money valuation, implying $7.5B. Peer-median analysis across 11 same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $2.3B, with a range of $1.0B to $23.0B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable; while the sector P/S median stands at 25.3x across the three public tickers, SambaNova's revenue is not disclosed, preventing reliable revenue-multiple application.

The triangulated range across these two methods is $2.0B (low) to $8.6B (high), with a median of $4.9B. The current $5.0B valuation sits near the median and reflects typical late-stage private multiples. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory relative to peers—none of which report consistent public disclosures—and stage-progression assumptions post-Series D, where capital intensity and path-to-exit vary substantially within semiconductor hardware.
